data.epi_content:
I thought it was all over, so even after hearing my brother's story, I just sat there silently.

I was simply too tired to respond anymore.

I was sick of this alien-like Crown Prince standing before me, who seemed to exist outside the normal boundaries of humanity.

"How about a more traditional method?

Stoning. A punishment given to women who lived in brothels or committed adultery hundreds of years ago.

Back then, it was something beyond conservative."

"Do I even have a choice in this matter?"

At my words, he pretended to consider it with exaggerated gestures, as if acknowledging my valid point.

"No, but I can make it seem like you do.

Though dying is dying, regardless of how."

"What would happen if I tried to escape from here?"

"The person who just severed your wrist would next cut off your ankle."

So there must be someone standing guard nearby.

Not an invisible person, but probably someone using magic to make themselves slightly transparent.

By the way, if they could even neatly clean up the corpse while I wasn't looking, there must be more than one person.

I wonder how many people usually follow him around.

"People are starting to gather around. Time to get back to work.

I suppose our conversation ends here. It was an interesting chat after a long time."

Looking around, I could hear murmuring voices gradually growing louder.

Outside the classroom, I could see a crowd in shock, staring at this scene.

Of course they would be.

If they heard a loud noise from the neighboring classroom and came to find a fellow noble dead with a hole in their head, and in the middle of it all, a beautiful woman (if I may say so myself) sitting and conversing with the Crown Prince, they'd naturally be shocked.

Did he say it was time to get back to work?

Does he consider being the Crown Prince not as something natural but as a kind of job?

While I was pondering this, the Crown Prince stood up and slapped my cheek with all his might.

My head turned from the impact, and as my body twisted, I hit my head against the wall.

"Ugh..."

"How dare you!! To commit such a terrible act in this sacred place established by the great founder..."

With a ringing in my ears, I couldn't hear clearly.

My vision was slightly shaky, but I could still see well enough.

Children had gathered, looking at the Crown Prince with admiring eyes.

And in the middle of them all, Vivian was staring at me with shocked eyes.

Ah, our eyes met.

She seemed to realize it too, as her body flinched.

For some reason, I wanted to reassure her.

I don't know why.

Anyway, I smiled, lifting the corners of my mouth.

With my body completely drained of strength, I collapsed there. Two men in military uniforms appeared from somewhere, wrapped purple cords around my hands and feet, and began dragging me away.

I was helplessly dragged along.

I don't remember much after that.

I just thought that the Crown Prince seemed to be doing his job well.

...............

When I opened my eyes, I was locked in a cell.

Of course, the purple cords that had bound my hands and feet were still wrapped around them.

I guess these were the things that prevented the use of magic.

There was a smell—whether it was food or food waste, I couldn't tell—so I looked ahead and saw a soup with a visual that suggested I shouldn't eat it.

Still, thinking the bread might be okay, I picked it up and took a bite, but it was so hard that I thought my teeth would break.
data.epi_content2:
So despite being hungry, I just threw the food on the floor.

This isn't something meant for human consumption.

I'm still wearing my school uniform.

I don't know if they were being considerate, but there are no other prisoners in the room, which gives me a sense of relief.

"Well, I'm bored."

Honestly, the concept of dying isn't something that really sinks in.

Even if I say I've died once, I don't know if I really died or if I just jumped back in time right before death.

In front of the cell bars, I can see a person with a curved sword at their waist.

When I got up and approached the bars, they took a long wooden club and jabbed it into my solar plexus.

I couldn't breathe.

"Cough! Hack!"

That's too much.

I thought about speaking to them, but they probably wouldn't answer, and if I heard something unpleasant, they might beat me with that club they just jabbed me with, so I held back.

Execution, huh.

I just hope they kill me painlessly.

As always, and like everyone else, I don't like pain.

Who would want to give others the right to take their life?

I'm only giving it up lightly because I'm confident I can't end my own life.

Still, I'm glad this world is a harsh one.

It's a beautiful and charming world only for the protagonist, Vivi-an, but it can impose fierce punishment on a terrible villain like me.

If Vivian had done the same thing I did—though that would never happen.

Anyway, it's just a hypothetical, so it doesn't matter.

Vivian would never be punished.

Instead, they would make Lydia out to be a wicked person who deserved to die, pin all sorts of filthy charges on her, and declare Vivian innocent.

Whether it's the Crown Prince, Evan, or nameless men.

Anyway, that's the kind of being she is.

A woman who seems to always live under divine protection.

They say humans need correction.

They said execution excludes the possibility of correction.

But what if that person doesn't want to be corrected?

What if their thoughts about life and their way of thinking about others have already hardened, making them unable to live any other way?

And if what they've already done is, in the eyes of others—specifically, in the eyes of the privileged class—unforgivable?

According to retributive justice, I must die.

But ironically, even though I committed this act with the intention of dying, I'm trembling with fear at the thought of what's coming.

Maybe I don't want to die after all.

And maybe that's because Lydia is gone from this world.

At least one person who tormented me is gone.

I could avoid Evan if needed, and if Vivian came looking for me, I could run away.

Why couldn't I think of anything other than the extreme method the Crown Prince mentioned?

Perhaps I was too cornered.

The reason a mouse bites a cat when cornered is not for some hopeful reason like survival.

With no presence of mind, feeling the immense threat of imminent death, experiencing the stress that comes with it, and seeing the creature that will torment me until death right in front of me, I just bit.

Because it seemed like there was no other way.

The miraculous thought of escaping the cat doesn't even cross my mind.

Mice are simple, stupid animals that value their lives greedily.

In that sense, I'm no different from a mouse.

Whenever I see danger, I never think about overcoming or defeating it; I just hurry to hide somewhere.

I get greedy when I see food I like.

I like the night when people don't walk around after the sun sets.
data.epi_content3:
I don't know if I like the darkness or if I like the absence of people.

When cornered, I bit Lydia like a mouse with sharp teeth.

The name of those teeth was a pistol.

When someone like Vivian approaches, it's obviously with goodwill, but I unnecessarily raise my guard and drive her away.

And it's the same now.

"Why did you come here? To mock me?"

Vivian looks like she's about to cry.

"I... I said I would help you.

Why... I said I would listen to anything you said..."

"Ah, that. You didn't keep your promise.

I think I told you to jump off, but well, what can we do?"

I didn't expect her to listen, and I had no intention of humbling myself to ask for favors.

In the end, Vivian cries in front of me.

But I'm the one who wants to cry.

I, no.

I don't want to cry.

After sobbing and hiccupping for a while, Vivian shares the message she came to deliver in a slightly hoarse voice.

"...His Highness said that the sentence will be carried out immediately after the verdict is announced at tomorrow's trial."

"And?"

"I begged him to save Erica, but he said it's impossible.

He said the Academy can only be maintained, albeit shakily, if you die.

No, I know it's impossible, but even though I understand it rationally, I can't accept it emotionally. I, I, what should I do?"

"We're not that close, you know."

"I thought we were!

Erica was the first female friend I made at the Academy, no.

Erica was the first female friend I made in my life.

Why did this happen?

Is it all because Evan likes me?

Should I tear off this face and live to prevent such things from happening?"

"Why are you asking me that?"

I stood up.

The person in military uniform holding the wooden club glared at me.

Perhaps they thought I might try to escape.

It's not like Vivian would open the door for me.

"But, Vivian.

Your future will always be bright, as always.

You won't end up in the gutter like me."

Saying that, I uttered words that this lady would never normally say, words that would make her have a fit.

Maybe it's because I'm about to die that my will, or whatever it is, has weakened.

"I envy you, I'm jealous of you.

That's probably why I dislike you."

"...What?"

"I'm just saying.

I'm about to die, so why can't I say what I want?"

Feeling annoyed, I wanted to throw something, but the floor was cold stone, and there wasn't even a piece of trash to throw, so I picked up the hard bread and threw it.
